passages.title: Cloze Test 17-21
passages.text: By the time the reinforcements arrived at the front lines, ----.

passages.title: Cloze Test 22-26
passages.text: Unlike his predecessor, who was known for his conservative approach, ----.

questions.question: Although the initial results of the clinical trial were promising, ----.

options.A: the research team decided to expand the scope of the study immediately
options.B: subsequent phases failed to demonstrate a statistically significant improvement in patient outcomes
options.C: many participants reported a high level of satisfaction with the treatment
options.D: the funding for the project has been renewed for another five years
options.E: it is widely expected that the drug will receive FDA approval shortly

questions.question: Even though urbanization brings about numerous economic opportunities, ----.

options.A: it often leads to the degradation of local ecosystems and increased social inequality
options.B: cities are becoming more sustainable through the use of renewable energy sources
options.C: the rural population is increasingly migrating to urban centers for better jobs
options.D: governments are providing more subsidies to support agricultural production
options.E: the cost of living in metropolitan areas is generally lower than in the countryside

questions.question: ---- because the fossil record for that specific era remains remarkably sparse.

options.A: Paleontologists have accurately mapped the evolution of the species
options.B: The extinction event can be attributed to a sudden climate shift
options.C: The exact cause of the species' decline is still a matter of conjecture
options.D: New discoveries have shed light on the migratory patterns of early mammals
options.E: Researchers have successfully sequenced the DNA of the extinct organism

questions.question: Unless international organizations implement stricter regulations on carbon emissions, ----.

options.A: the global temperature is likely to rise beyond the critical threshold
options.B: renewable energy will become the primary source of power by 2050
options.C: most countries will meet their climate targets ahead of schedule
options.D: the impact of global warming on coastal cities will be minimized
options.E: private companies will voluntarily reduce their environmental footprint

questions.question: ---- so that they could better understand the long-term effects of the policy on the marginalized communities.

options.A: The government ignored the recommendations made by the sociologists
options.B: A comprehensive longitudinal study was commissioned by the research institute
options.C: Most citizens were unaware of the changes in the legislative framework
options.D: The economic indicators showed a steady growth over the last decade
options.E: Advocacy groups protested against the lack of transparency in the decision-making process

questions.question: Just as the invention of the printing press revolutionized the spread of knowledge in the 15th century, ----.

options.A: the internet has transformed the way information is disseminated and consumed today
options.B: books were once considered a luxury item only for the elite
options.C: literacy rates have remained stagnant in many developing countries
options.D: it was difficult to mass-produce documents before the modern era
options.E: the Renaissance was a period of great cultural and intellectual growth

questions.question: Despite the fact that the architecture of the building is strikingly modern, ----.

options.A: it incorporates traditional materials that reflect the local heritage
options.B: the interior design is equally avant-garde and innovative
options.C: it has won several international awards for its sustainable design
options.D: it was completed well ahead of the scheduled deadline
options.E: the cost of construction exceeded the initial budget estimates

questions.question: While some critics argue that artificial intelligence will eventually replace human labor, ----.

options.A: others believe it will augment human capabilities and create new job sectors
options.B: the technology is still in its infancy and cannot perform complex tasks
options.C: it is already being used in various industries such as healthcare and finance
options.D: companies are investing heavily in AI research and development
options.E: the ethical implications of AI are being debated globally

questions.question: Since the tectonic plates in this region are constantly shifting, ----.

options.A: the area is prone to frequent seismic activity and volcanic eruptions
options.B: geologists have concluded that the risk of an earthquake is minimal
options.C: the landscape has remained unchanged for millions of years
options.D: construction projects are exempt from strict building codes
options.E: local residents are largely unaware of the geological hazards

questions.question: The philosopher’s argument was so complex and nuanced ----.

options.A: that even his most dedicated students struggled to grasp its full implications
options.B: so that it could be easily understood by the general public
options.C: than the previous theories proposed by his contemporaries
options.D: because it relied on empirical evidence rather than abstract reasoning
options.E: whether it was accepted by the academic community or not

questions.question: ---- provided that the central bank maintains its current stance on interest rates.

options.A: Economic growth is expected to remain stable throughout the next quarter
options.B: Inflation had already reached an all-time high last year
options.C: The stock market crashed due to unforeseen global events
options.D: Investors were reluctant to commit their capital to long-term projects
options.E: The government will likely increase taxes to cover the budget deficit

questions.question: No matter how much the pharmaceutical company invested in marketing, ----.

options.A: the drug failed to gain significant market share due to its high price
options.B: it was eventually banned by the health authorities for safety reasons
options.C: the sales figures exceeded all previous projections
options.D: it became the most prescribed medication in its category
options.E: competitors were unable to replicate its success

questions.question: The primary goal of the new educational reform is to foster critical thinking skills ----.

options.A: rather than focusing solely on the rote memorization of facts
options.B: in order to ensure that students pass their standardized tests
options.C: so that teachers can have more control over the curriculum
options.D: because the previous system was considered highly effective
options.E: although the budget for the program has been significantly reduced

questions.question: In view of the increasing scarcity of freshwater resources, ----.

options.A: desalination plants are being built in many arid regions despite their high cost
options.B: most people continue to waste water in their daily routines
options.C: the government has decided to lower the price of water for industrial use
options.D: scientists have proven that the earth's water cycle is self-sustaining
options.E: the agricultural sector is shifting towards water-intensive crops

questions.question: Hardly had the treaty been signed ----.

options.A: when border skirmishes between the two nations broke out again
options.B: than the diplomat returned to his home country for a vacation
options.C: because both sides were satisfied with the outcome of the negotiations
options.D: the international community praised the peace agreement
options.E: so that the trade relations could be restored immediately

questions.question: ---- whereas others maintain that intelligence is primarily determined by genetic factors.

options.A: Some researchers argue that environmental influences play a crucial role in cognitive development
options.B: The debate over the nature versus nurture theory has been settled
options.C: It is widely accepted that both genes and environment are equally important
options.D: Psychologists have identified several different types of intelligence
options.E: The study of human behavior has become increasingly multidisciplinary

questions.question: Boşluk 17 için en uygun seçeneği bulun.

options.A: the battle had already been lost by the defending army
options.B: they were well-equipped and ready for the upcoming offensive
options.C: the commander decided to postpone the attack until dawn
options.D: a ceasefire was being negotiated by the neutral parties
options.E: the soldiers were exhausted from their long journey

questions.question: Boşluk 18 için en uygun seçeneği bulun.

options.A: The surgeons meticulously reviewed the patient's medical history
options.B: The hospital decided to increase the cost of the procedure
options.C: The patient was discharged immediately after the operation
options.D: New protocols were developed to speed up the recovery process
options.E: The surgical team ignored the standard safety precautions

questions.question: Boşluk 19 için en uygun seçeneği bulun.

options.A: that it disrupted satellite communications across the entire planet
options.B: than any other event recorded in the history of astronomy
options.C: because the sun is currently at its peak activity cycle
options.D: so that scientists could observe it through their telescopes
options.E: whether it would affect the Earth's magnetic field remained to be seen

questions.question: Boşluk 20 için en uygun seçeneği bulun.

options.A: the authorities will have to evacuate thousands of residents in the vicinity
options.B: it had been dormant for several centuries without any signs of life
options.C: the local economy would have suffered a major blow from the loss of tourism
options.D: scientists are monitoring the seismic activity around the clock
options.E: the ash cloud would block out the sun for several weeks

questions.question: Boşluk 21 için en uygun seçeneği bulun.

options.A: The suspect claimed that he was at home during the time of the robbery
options.B: The police are convinced that they have arrested the right person
options.C: The witness testimony confirmed the suspect's alibi
options.D: Forensic experts were unable to find any DNA traces
options.E: The jury reached a unanimous verdict of guilty

questions.question: Boşluk 22 için en uygun seçeneği bulun.

options.A: the new CEO is more inclined to take risks and innovate
options.B: the company has seen a steady decline in its profits
options.C: he had served in the board of directors for over a decade
options.D: the employees were used to a more traditional style of management
options.E: the industry is facing unprecedented challenges from competitors

questions.question: Boşluk 23 için en uygun seçeneği bulun.

options.A: Anthropologists are rushing to document the endangered languages
options.B: Most people are becoming bilingual in the official language and their mother tongue
options.C: The government has introduced new policies to promote cultural heritage
options.D: Children are no longer learning the traditional songs and stories
options.E: Language technology is being used to revitalize ancient scripts

questions.question: Boşluk 24 için en uygun seçeneği bulun.

options.A: is still a subject of intense debate among some scientists
options.B: the consensus is that urgent action is needed to mitigate its effects
options.C: it has already led to the melting of the polar ice caps
options.D: governments are reluctant to implement carbon taxes
options.E: the impact on biodiversity will be catastrophic in the long run

questions.question: Boşluk 25 için en uygun seçeneği bulun.

options.A: but he also established several trading posts along the coast
options.B: than he returned to his homeland as a national hero
options.C: as he was also a skilled cartographer and navigator
options.D: because he wanted to expand the influence of his empire
options.E: so that future generations could benefit from his findings

questions.question: Boşluk 26 için en uygun seçeneği bulun.

options.A: The military commander decided to encrypt all communication
options.B: The journalists published the report without any redactions
options.C: The public was fully informed about the details of the operation
options.D: The spies were able to infiltrate the high-security facility
options.E: The government declassified the documents after fifty years

questions.question: Much as the public admires the actor's talent, ----.

options.A: his controversial personal life often overshadows his professional achievements
options.B: he has won numerous awards for his performances in independent films
options.C: everyone was excited to see his latest blockbuster movie
options.D: he is known for his philanthropic work in developing countries
options.E: his latest play has received rave reviews from the critics

questions.question: If the current trend of urbanization continues at this pace, ----.

options.A: megacities will face immense pressure on their infrastructure and resources
options.B: the rural areas will benefit from increased investment in agriculture
options.C: the air quality in urban centers will improve significantly
options.D: most people will choose to live in the suburbs for a better quality of life
options.E: the government will be forced to ban migration to the cities

questions.question: ---- lest the audience should lose interest in the lengthy performance.

options.A: The director decided to cut several scenes from the final act
options.B: The actors were encouraged to improvise their lines
options.C: The play was advertised as a groundbreaking masterpiece
options.D: The tickets were sold at a discounted price for students
options.E: The theater was equipped with the latest sound technology

questions.question: The experiment yielded results that were consistent with the hypothesis; however, ----.

options.A: the sample size was too small to draw a definitive conclusion
options.B: the researchers were surprised by the accuracy of their predictions
options.C: further studies are needed to replicate the findings
options.D: the data was published in a prestigious peer-reviewed journal
options.E: it confirmed what had been suspected by the scientific community

questions.question: In spite of the significant advances in medical technology, ----.

options.A: many infectious diseases remain a major threat to global health
options.B: the average life expectancy has increased in most countries
options.C: doctors are now able to perform surgeries with high precision
options.D: vaccines have successfully eradicated several deadly viruses
options.E: the cost of healthcare has become more affordable for the poor

questions.question: ---- as if the entire city had been frozen in time.

options.A: The ruins of the ancient civilization were remarkably well-preserved
options.B: The modern skyscrapers were designed with a futuristic aesthetic
options.C: The bustling market was filled with people from all over the world
options.D: The city was rapidly expanding into the surrounding countryside
options.E: The residents were preparing for the annual festival

questions.question: So long as the conflict remains unresolved, ----.

options.A: the region will continue to suffer from instability and economic decline
options.B: peace talks are being held in a neutral third country
options.C: both sides have agreed to a temporary ceasefire
options.D: the international community has provided humanitarian aid
options.E: the neighboring countries are bracing for a surge in refugees

questions.question: Despite being one of the most brilliant minds of his time, ----.

options.A: he lived a life of relative obscurity and financial hardship
options.B: his theories were immediately accepted by the scientific community
options.C: he was appointed as the head of the royal academy
options.D: his work has had a profound impact on modern physics
options.E: he was known for his outgoing and charismatic personality

questions.question: The more we learn about the complexities of the human brain, ----.

options.A: the more we realize how much is still left to be discovered
options.B: it becomes easier to treat neurological disorders
options.C: neuroscientists are using advanced imaging techniques
options.D: the brain is capable of adapting to new information
options.E: it is clear that the mind is separate from the body

questions.question: ---- on the grounds that the evidence was obtained through illegal means.

options.A: The judge dismissed the charges against the defendant
options.B: The prosecutor presented a compelling case to the jury
options.C: The witness was cross-examined by the defense attorney
options.D: The police conducted a thorough investigation of the crime
options.E: The suspect confessed to the crime during the interrogation

questions.question: While the internet has democratized access to information, ----.

options.A: it has also facilitated the spread of misinformation and fake news
options.B: it is now easier than ever to learn a new skill online
options.C: most people use social media to stay connected with friends
options.D: governments are trying to regulate online content
options.E: it has revolutionized the way we conduct business

questions.question: Such a drastic reduction in biodiversity ----.

options.A: could lead to the collapse of entire ecosystems and food chains
options.B: is being addressed by international conservation efforts
options.C: was caused by the introduction of invasive species
options.D: has been observed in tropical rainforests over the last decade
options.E: can be reversed through habitat restoration projects

questions.question: ---- as though she had known the secret for her entire life.

options.A: She spoke with a confidence that surprised everyone in the room
options.B: She was hesitant to share her thoughts with the rest of the group
options.C: She looked shocked when the truth was finally revealed
options.D: She had been searching for the answer for many years
options.E: She refused to believe the news when she first heard it

questions.question: The success of the mission depends not only on the quality of the equipment ----.

options.A: but also on the expertise and coordination of the crew members
options.B: than on the amount of funding received from the government
options.C: as it does on the weather conditions at the launch site
options.D: because the stakes are higher than they have ever been
options.E: so that the objectives can be achieved within the timeframe

questions.question: Whereas some species have adapted to the harsh conditions of the desert, ----.

options.A: others have perished due to the lack of water and extreme heat
options.B: the desert ecosystem is more diverse than previously thought
options.C: cacti are able to store water in their stems for long periods
options.D: camels are well-suited for traveling across the sand dunes
options.E: the nocturnal animals emerge only after the sun goes down

questions.question: ---- since the available data does not support such a broad generalization.

options.A: The researchers' conclusion has been met with significant skepticism
options.B: The study has been hailed as a breakthrough in the field
options.C: Most experts agree that the findings are conclusive
options.D: The hypothesis was confirmed by multiple independent trials
options.E: The results were published in a top-tier academic journal

questions.question: Had the government taken proactive measures earlier, ----.

options.A: the economic crisis could have been significantly mitigated
options.B: the situation is currently being monitored by the experts
options.C: they would have known that the policies were ineffective
options.D: it is now too late to prevent the impending disaster
options.E: the public is demanding more transparency from the officials

questions.question: The novel is widely regarded as a masterpiece of modern literature, ----.

options.A: even though it was initially rejected by several publishers
options.B: because it tells the story of a young man's journey
options.C: and it has been translated into more than fifty languages
options.D: although the author died before it was ever finished
options.E: as it explores themes of identity and belonging

questions.question: ---- so that the researchers could ensure the validity of their findings.

options.A: The experiment was conducted under strictly controlled conditions
options.B: The data was collected from a diverse group of participants
options.C: The results were shared with the international scientific community
options.D: The funding for the project was provided by a private foundation
options.E: The software was updated to the latest version before the analysis

questions.question: In light of the recent scandals involving data privacy, ----.

options.A: many users are becoming more cautious about their online activities
options.B: technology companies are reporting record-breaking profits
options.C: the government has decided to deregulate the tech industry
options.D: social media platforms are gaining more popularity than ever
options.E: it is now easier for hackers to access personal information

questions.question: ---- that it is virtually impossible to predict the exact timing of the event.

options.A: The complexity of the variables involved is so great
options.B: The scientists have developed a highly accurate model
options.C: The historical records provide a clear pattern of occurrences
options.D: The technology has advanced to a point where we can see it
options.E: The researchers are confident in their latest projections

questions.question: The diplomat's speech was carefully worded ----.

options.A: to avoid offending either of the two conflicting parties
options.B: so that it could be easily translated into other languages
options.C: because he wanted to express his personal opinion on the matter
options.D: than the one he had given at the previous summit
options.E: as if he were trying to provoke a strong reaction from the crowd

questions.question: Even if the new tax policy is implemented next year, ----.

options.A: it is unlikely to solve the country's long-term fiscal problems
options.B: the revenue will be used to fund public infrastructure projects
options.C: most businesses have already adjusted their budgets accordingly
options.D: the opposition party is planning to challenge it in court
options.E: the inflation rate has been decreasing steadily over the months

questions.question: ---- but the archaeological evidence indicates that the civilization was quite advanced.

options.A: Some historians once believed that the tribe was primitive
options.B: The city was destroyed by a massive earthquake in the past
options.C: The artifacts found in the tomb are made of gold and silver
options.D: The script used by the people has not yet been deciphered
options.E: The ruins are located in a remote and inaccessible region
